spotify.name: Boris Godunov: Opera in Four Acts With a Prologue: Prologue, Scene 2, Introduction

playlist_tracks.connection: "Boris"; Plan B references Boris Johnson, leading to Boris Godunov opera.

playlist_tracks.name: King
playlist_tracks.connection: Coronation and kingship; featured on King Charles III's official Coronation playlist (2023).

playlist_tracks.name: In the Hall of the Mountain King

spotify.name: Peer Gynt, Op. 23: Act II, 7, In the Hall of the Mountain King

playlist_tracks.connection: Melodic similarity; the hook/riff resembles "In the Hall of the Mountain King" when slowed down.

playlist_tracks.name: Heaven
playlist_tracks.connection: Composed/produced in a shed; Grieg wrote in his shed, Naughty Boy produced in his parents' garden shed.

playlist_tracks.name: Chasing Pavements
playlist_tracks.connection: Stage names; the artist's real name is Adele Laurie Blue Adkins, and the track continues a theme of artists who changed their names.

playlist_tracks.name: Mood Indigo
playlist_tracks.connection: The word "Blue" in Adele's full name links to jazz "blue notes" and the opening lyric of "Mood Indigo" ("You ain't been blue, no, no, no")

playlist_tracks.name: Tuba mirum

spotify.name: Requiem, K. 626 (Ed. Beyer/Levin): IIIb. Tuba mirum - Live

playlist_tracks.connection: Prominent trombone solos.

playlist_tracks.name: Back Together Again
playlist_tracks.connection: Posthumous completion; Mozart's "Requiem" was finished after his death; "Back Together Again" was completed by Roberta Flack after Donny Hathaway's death.

playlist_tracks.name: Killing Me Softly With His Song

spotify.name: Killing Me Softly With His Song

playlist_tracks.connection: Cover of Roberta Flack's "Killing Me Softly", reimagined as a hip-hop track.

playlist_tracks.name: Louie Louie
playlist_tracks.connection: Written on whatever surface was available: "Killing Me Softly" on a napkin, "Louie Louie" on a scrap of toilet paper.

playlist_tracks.name: I Will Survive
playlist_tracks.connection: Both originally appeared as B-sides ("Louie Louie" as B-side to "Haunted Castle", "I Will Survive" as B-side to "Substitute").

playlist_tracks.name: Organ Sonata No.4 movt II (Andante)

spotify.name: Organ Sonata No. 4, BWV 528: II. Andante [Adagio] (Transcr. by August Stradal)

playlist_tracks.connection: Based on the circle of fifths chord progression.

playlist_tracks.name: My Dearest Dear by Ivor Novello –
playlist_tracks.connection: Both composers spent time in jail (Bach for resigning from his position as Konzertmeister, Novello for misuse of petrol coupons).

playlist_tracks.name: WHO AM I
playlist_tracks.connection: Won the Ivor Novello Award for Best Album, named after Ivor Novello.

playlist_tracks.name: Calypso Queen
playlist_tracks.connection: Builds from Berwyn's Trinidadian origins to another Trinidadian artist, Calypso Rose, exploring themes of identity and resilience from the Caribbean diaspora.

playlist_tracks.name: Concrete and Clay
playlist_tracks.connection: Uses the same bayou rhythm (clave rhythm) as Calypso Rose's music, which was popularized in 1970s Western pop.

playlist_tracks.name: Piano Concerto in G Major: III (Rondo)

spotify.name: Piano Concerto No. 4 in G Major, Op. 58: III. Rondo. Vivace

playlist_tracks.connection: The music video for "Concrete and Clay" was filmed at the construction site of the Barbican Centre; Beethoven's Piano Concerto No. 4 was performed at the Barbican's opening concert in 1982.

playlist_tracks.name: Blue Rondo à la Turk
playlist_tracks.connection: Rondo form (musical form with a recurring main theme), with Brubeck's piece being a jazz interpretation of the classical rondo structure.

playlist_tracks.name: Gangnam Style
playlist_tracks.connection: Dave Brubeck was a U.S. cultural ambassador in the 1950s; Psy became a 21st-century cultural ambassador for South Korea, with both using music as soft power.

playlist_tracks.name: Hocus Pocus
playlist_tracks.connection: Speed, and virtuosic/fast-paced playing.

playlist_tracks.name: Teardrop
playlist_tracks.connection: Bands using self-sufficient, off-grid power sources on tour.

playlist_tracks.name: Glory to God in the Highest

spotify.name: Messiah: Part 1, Glory to God in the highest (Chorus)

playlist_tracks.connection: Themes of birth and celebration, inspired by the "Teardrop" video featuring a fetus.

playlist_tracks.name: Animal Crackers
playlist_tracks.connection: Audience performance traditions (standing for Handel's "Hallelujah" chorus; lighting candles/lighters, which started at Melanie's Woodstock performance).

playlist_tracks.name: Feel Good Inc
playlist_tracks.connection: Laughter used as an instrumental or lyrical element.

playlist_tracks.name: Main Title Theme from Batman
playlist_tracks.connection: Bass line from "Feel Good Inc" played down a third matches the opening riff of the Batman theme.

playlist_tracks.name: Another One Bites the Dust (live)

spotify.name: Another One Bites the Dust - Live At Wembley Stadium / July 1986

playlist_tracks.connection: Filming location (Wayne Manor) was Knebworth House, which also hosted the Queen concert.

playlist_tracks.name: One Bass Hit
playlist_tracks.connection: Bass player wrote the track.

playlist_tracks.name: Posee un Corazón
playlist_tracks.connection: Dizzy Gillespie was a follower of the Baháʼí faith; this track is by a Baháʼí musician inspired by its teachings.

playlist_tracks.name: Runaway
playlist_tracks.connection: Family bands; The Corrs are a multi-instrumentalist Irish family band, similar to Leonor Dely's family group.

playlist_tracks.name: La flûte de Pan

spotify.name: Trois chansons de Bilitis, L. 90: No. 1, La flûte de Pan

playlist_tracks.connection: Modal harmony; focuses on the Lydian mode, specifically the raised fourth degree heard at the end of "Runaway".

playlist_tracks.name: Cassandra
playlist_tracks.connection: Greek myths in music; inspired by the fabricated ancient Greek poetry story behind the Debussy piece, linking to the myth of Cassandra.

playlist_tracks.name: Stay With Me
playlist_tracks.connection: Music about predicting the future; connects the Cassandra myth to film scores dealing with precognition.

playlist_tracks.name: Ya Taali’een el-Jabal
playlist_tracks.connection: The Kronos Quartet; continues from their appearance on the Clint Mansell track, highlighting their genre-bending versatility and recent collaboration.

comments: Unusually, the same musical group (the Kronos Quartet) appears in two consecutive tracks.

playlist_tracks.name: Unforgettable
playlist_tracks.connection: Uses the recorded voice of a deceased person (Nat King Cole) to create a new duet with his daughter, Natalie Cole, paralleling the posthumous vocal use in the previous episode's track.

playlist_tracks.name: Señor Mouse
playlist_tracks.connection: The prominent use of the vibraphone.

playlist_tracks.name: La Cathédrale Engloutie

spotify.name: Préludes / Book 1, L.117: 10. La cathédrale engloutie - Live

playlist_tracks.connection: Explores parallel fourths and fifths (chordal harmonies) heard in "Señor Mouse", a technique popularised in classical music by Debussy.
playlist_tracks.comments: Debussy picked for two consecutive episodes.

playlist_tracks.name: Stingray
playlist_tracks.connection: Underwater theme, inspired by the submerged cathedral imagery in Debussy's piece. Links to the 1960s puppet TV series "Stingray" set in an undersea world.

playlist_tracks.name: The Lonely Goatherd
playlist_tracks.connection: Continues the puppet theme from "Stingray"; the song is performed with puppets in the film "The Sound of Music".

playlist_tracks.name: Wolf Totem
playlist_tracks.connection: Vocal techniques; yodeling in "The Lonely Goatherd" and Mongolian throat singing in "Wolf Totem".

playlist_tracks.name: Daybreak (Lever du jour)

spotify.name: Daphnis et Chloé, M. 57, Pt. 3: X. Lever du jour "Daybreak"

playlist_tracks.connection: Nature-inspired soundscapes and layered, evolving orchestration evoking a sunrise.

playlist_tracks.name: Stranger Things Theme
playlist_tracks.connection: Repetitive, looping arpeggiated patterns in the flutes (Ravel) compared to the synthesised arpeggiator in the theme.

playlist_tracks.name: Madeline
playlist_tracks.connection: Actor David Harbour, who stars in "Stranger Things", is married to Lily Allen and features in her album.

playlist_tracks.name: Ex-Wives
playlist_tracks.connection: Musical set in London's West End, linking to Lily Allen's album title "West End Girl"; theme of public breakups.

playlist_tracks.name: Sinfonia from The Christmas Oratorio

spotify.name: Christmas Oratorio, BWV 248, Pt. 2: No. 10, Sinfonia

playlist_tracks.connection: Space theme; both Ella Fitzgerald and Bach have had music used as astronaut wake-up calls.

playlist_tracks.artist: The Sixteen, written by Mykola Leontovych and Peter Wilhousky
playlist_tracks.name: Carol of the Bells
playlist_tracks.connection: Bach's reuse of secular music for the oratorio; this piece also began as a non-Christmas folk tune.

playlist_tracks.name: Step into Christmas
playlist_tracks.connection: Bells; moving from vocal imitation of bells to prominent use of sleigh bells and tubular bells.

playlist_tracks.name: Feliz Navidad
playlist_tracks.connection: Celebrating winter celebrations worldwide; shifting from a Christmas pop song to a bilingual Christmas classic from Puerto Rico.

playlist_tracks.name: Love’s Dream
playlist_tracks.connection: Theme of love (track title translates to "love's dream"); Liszt as a romantic figure akin to a rock star.

playlist_tracks.name: Thor: Ragnarok
playlist_tracks.connection: Rick Wakeman's role as Thor in the film "Lisztomania" leads to the Marvel superhero Thor and its theme music.

playlist_tracks.name: Allegro moderato from Violin Concerto No 1 in B-flat Major

spotify.name: Mozart: Violin Concerto No. 1 in B-Flat Major, K. 207: I. Allegro moderato (Cadenza by Cohen)

playlist_tracks.connection: Composer Mark Mothersbaugh's openness about having ADHD connects to speculation that Mozart was neurodivergent; both composers display humor and quirkiness in their music.

playlist_tracks.name: The Outside
playlist_tracks.connection: Mozart composed music from age five; Taylor Swift wrote "The Outside" at age twelve, highlighting prodigious young talent.

playlist_tracks.name: Do I Ever Cross Your Mind
playlist_tracks.connection: Geographical link to Nashville, where Taylor Swift moved to pursue her music career; both artists associated with the Grand Ole Opry and country music scene.

spotify.name: Do I Ever Cross Your Mind

playlist_tracks.name: Tre sbirri, una carrozza...Te Deum
playlist_tracks.connection: Dolly Parton's Welsh ancestry (Conwy) connects to Welsh bass-baritone Bryn Terfel, who sang the role of Scarpia in Puccini's "Tosca".

spotify.name: Tosca, Act I: Tre sbirri, una carrozza, presto – Te Deum

playlist_tracks.name: Sober
playlist_tracks.connection: The plot of "Tosca" spans roughly 18 hours from afternoon to dawn; Lorde's album "Melodrama" tracks a single house party from evening into the following morning.

playlist_tracks.name: Memory
playlist_tracks.connection: The word "midnight" in Lorde's "Sober" ("midnight, lose my mind") links to "Memory" from the musical "Cats", which begins with the line "Midnight, not a sound from the pavement".

playlist_tracks.name: With Love
playlist_tracks.connection: The first commercial release of "Memory" was an instrumental by Irish guitarist Gary Moore, who was a member of Thin Lizzy; both tracks share Irish rock and blues influences.

playlist_tracks.name: Hips Don’t Lie
playlist_tracks.connection: Both musicians have been honored with large bronze statues: Phil Lynott (Thin Lizzy) in Dublin, and Shakira in her hometown of Barranquilla, Colombia.

playlist_tracks.name: Zithande
playlist_tracks.connection: Shakira performed "Hips Don't Lie" at the 2006 World Cup closing ceremony; she later co-wrote and performed the official 2010 World Cup song "Waka Waka (This Time for Africa)" with South African band Freshlyground.

playlist_tracks.name: Another Brick in the Wall, Part 2,
playlist_tracks.connection: Use of a children's choir in "Zithande" to amplify emotional weight; similar use of a children's choir in Pink Floyd's "Another Brick in the Wall, Part 2" as a protest against educational oppression.

spotify.name: Another Brick in the Wall, Pt. 2

playlist_tracks.name: String Quartet No 8, 2nd mvt: Allegro Molto
playlist_tracks.connection: Protagonist and protest theme in Pink Floyd's song leads to Shostakovich as a reluctant hero using his musical signature (DSCH cryptogram) to protest under the Soviet regime without lyrics.

spotify.name: String Quartet No. 8 in C Minor, Op. 110: II. Allegro molto

playlist_tracks.name: Big Yellow Taxi
playlist_tracks.connection: Shostakovich's quartet was partly inspired by the Allied bombing of Dresden; Joni Mitchell's "Big Yellow Taxi" laments environmental destruction, specifically the loss of natural beauty to urban development in Hawaii.

playlist_tracks.name: Etudes: No 6
playlist_tracks.connection: Both linked to taxis: Joni Mitchell's "Big Yellow Taxi" leads to Philip Glass, who worked as a taxi driver in New York while building his music career.

playlist_tracks.name: Pyramid Song
playlist_tracks.connection: Metric displacement in Glass's Etude No. 6 is also a key feature in Radiohead's "Pyramid Song," where the rhythm disorients the listener.

playlist_tracks.name: Hassle Attack
playlist_tracks.connection: Radiohead's Thom Yorke started playing piano in his early 30s; blues artist Ann Rabson also began playing piano in her mid-30s after establishing herself as a guitarist.

playlist_tracks.name: Ballaké Sissoko
playlist_tracks.connection: Blues has roots in African music and the pentatonic scale; Sona Jobarteh is a virtuoso of the kora (West African harp) and the first prominent female kora player in a male-dominated tradition.

playlist_tracks.name: On the Road Again (Live)
playlist_tracks.connection: Sona Jobarteh's musical lineage comes through her grandfather (Amadu Bansang Jobarteh); Willie Nelson was raised by his paternal grandparents, who fostered his musical education and gave him his first guitar at age six.

playlist_tracks.name: Sexual Healing
playlist_tracks.connection: Both Marvin Gaye and Willie Nelson recorded albums to pay off substantial debts to the IRS (Gaye's "Midnight Love" and Nelson's "The IRS Tapes: Who'll Buy My Memories?").

playlist_tracks.name: Clocks
playlist_tracks.connection: The habanera rhythm (1-2-3-1-2) in Marvin Gaye's "Sexual Healing" is the same rhythmic pattern that drives the piano riff in Coldplay's "Clocks".

playlist_tracks.name: The Clock (the 2nd mvt) from Symphony No 101 in D Major

spotify.name: Symphony No. 101 in D Major, Hob. I:101 "The Clock": II. Andante

playlist_tracks.connection: Coldplay's "Clocks" uses a repeating piano motif to express the idea of time; Haydn's Symphony No. 101, second movement, is nicknamed "The Clock" and depicts ticking through rhythm and orchestration.

playlist_tracks.name: Precipitato (the 3rd mvt) from Piano Sonata No 7 in B Flat Major

spotify.name: Prokofiev: Piano Sonata No. 7 in B-Flat Major, Op. 83: III. Precipitato

playlist_tracks.connection: Repetition as a musical device: Haydn's gentle ticking clock contrasts with Prokofiev's relentless, menacing repeated figures in the third movement of Piano Sonata No. 7 ("Precipitato"), written during WWII.

playlist_tracks.name: Gravel Pit

spotify.name: Gravel Pit (feat. RZA, Method Man, Ghostface Killah, Raekwon & U-God)

playlist_tracks.connection: Prokofiev was a master-level chess player who defeated a world champion; RZA (of Wu-Tang Clan) is a devoted chess player, director of the Hip-Hop Chess Federation, and incorporates chess references into his music and production strategy.

playlist_tracks.name: Thunderstruck
playlist_tracks.connection: Abbreviations: RZA (of Wu-Tang Clan) stands for "Ruler, Zig-Zag-Zig, Allah"; AC/DC stands for "Alternating Current/Direct Current", taken from a sewing machine.

playlist_tracks.name: I Got You (I Feel Good)
playlist_tracks.connection: Signature stage moves: Angus Young of AC/DC performs a one-legged hopping move while playing guitar, inspired by Chuck Berry and James Brown, whose signature moves included leaping into the splits and using the microphone stand as an accessory.

playlist_tracks.name: Hommage à Rameau

spotify.name: Images I, CD 105: II. Hommage à Rameau

playlist_tracks.connection: The dominant ninth chord appears in James Brown's "I Got You (I Feel Good)" and also features prominently in Debussy's "Hommage à Rameau", creating harmonic ambiguity and a floating quality.

playlist_tracks.name: My Reverie
playlist_tracks.connection: Debussy's harmonic language inspired jazz musicians; Larry Clinton based his song "My Reverie" on Debussy's "Rêverie", keeping the melody virtually identical, and Ella Fitzgerald recorded a famous version.

playlist_tracks.name: Peter Piper
playlist_tracks.connection: Nursery rhymes: Ella Fitzgerald's first breakout hit in 1938 was "A-Tisket, A-Tasket", based on a children's nursery rhyme; Run-D.M.C.'s "Peter Piper" samples and references at least eight nursery rhymes and children's stories.

playlist_tracks.name: Mercedes Benz
playlist_tracks.connection: Commercial relationships with brands: Run-D.M.C. signed a $1.6 million endorsement deal with Adidas; Janis Joplin's "Mercedes Benz" critiques consumerism but was later used in a Mercedes ad.

playlist_tracks.name: Stabat Mater Dolorosa
playlist_tracks.connection: Untimely death at a young age: Janis Joplin died at 27; Pergolesi died at 26 while writing his "Stabat Mater", which became his most famous piece.

spotify.name: Pergolesi: Stabat Mater: I. Stabat mater dolorosa

playlist_tracks.name: Mambo Bacan
playlist_tracks.connection: Geographical location: Pergolesi died in Pozzuoli, Italy; Sophia Loren grew up there as a child, and "Mambo Bacan" is from her early career.

playlist_tracks.name: The Pink Panther Theme
playlist_tracks.connection: Sophia Loren had a relationship with Peter Sellers; Sellers starred as Inspector Clouseau in "The Pink Panther", for which Henry Mancini composed the iconic theme.

spotify.name: The Pink Panther Theme - From "The Pink Panther"

playlist_tracks.name: Say My Name
playlist_tracks.connection: Grammy Awards: Henry Mancini had 72 nominations and 20 wins; Beyoncé (of Destiny's Child) holds the record for most Grammy wins (35) and most nominations (99), and "Say My Name" won two Grammys in 2001.

playlist_tracks.name: An American in Paris

spotify.name: An American in Paris (Ed. M. Clauge) - Live

playlist_tracks.connection: Both feature city soundscapes with car horns (real taxi horns in Gershwin, recorded car horns and pneumatic drill in The Lovin' Spoonful).

playlist_tracks.name: Overture to Phaëton
playlist_tracks.connection: Cultural hub of Paris as a setting; an Italian in Paris (Lully) following an American in Paris (Gershwin).

playlist_tracks.name: Ain’t It Strange
playlist_tracks.connection: Musicians injured on stage: Lully stabbed his foot with a conducting staff; Patti Smith fell off stage, breaking vertebrae and skull.

playlist_tracks.name: Violin Partita No. 2 in D Minor (the Chaconne)

spotify.name: Violin Partita No. 2 in D Minor, BWV 1004: V. Chaconne (Arr. J. Milone for Violin & Orchestra)

artists.name: Academy of St. Martin in the Fields

playlist_tracks.connection: Busking: Patti Smith busked in Paris (1969) and Manhattan (2020); Joshua Bell (performing Bach's Chaconne) busked in a Washington D.C. subway station.

playlist_tracks.name: Mess Around
playlist_tracks.connection: Both Bach and Ray Charles were orphaned as children (Bach at 10, Ray Charles at 15).

playlist_tracks.name: Kyrie from the Mass in C Minor

spotify.name: Mass in C Minor, K. 427 "Grosse Messe" (Rev. and Reconstr. A. Schmitt & E. Gardiner): 1. Kyrie

playlist_tracks.connection: Genius and biopics: Ray Charles (film "Ray") and Mozart (film "Amadeus").

playlist_tracks.name: From the Morning
playlist_tracks.connection: Both Mozart and Nick Drake created profoundly meaningful work at age 26.

playlist_tracks.name: Fields of Gold
playlist_tracks.connection: Posthumous success: Nick Drake sold few copies in his lifetime but became hugely streamed; Eva Cassidy only found global fame after her death.

playlist_tracks.name: Proud Mary
playlist_tracks.connection: Covers that outshine or rival the original: Eva Cassidy's "Fields of Gold" (Sting cover) and Ike & Tina Turner's "Proud Mary" (Credence Clearwater Revival cover).

playlist_tracks.name: Quartet from Rigoletto
playlist_tracks.connection: Steamboats: the "Proud Mary" was a real steam-powered boat, and a steamboat features prominently in the film "Fitzcarraldo", which uses Verdi's music.

playlist_tracks.name: La Flor de la Canela
playlist_tracks.connection: Peru and its rivers: the Amazon river system in "Fitzcarraldo" and the Rímac river in Lima, celebrated in Chabuca Granda's song "La Flor de la Canela".

playlist_tracks.name: The Minute Waltz

spotify.name: Waltz No. 6 in D-Flat Major, Op. 64 No. 1 "Minute" - Remastered

playlist_tracks.connection: The waltz: Chabuca Granda's "Vals Criollo" (Peruvian waltz) adapts European waltzes, exemplified by Chopin's "Minute Waltz".

playlist_tracks.name: My Native Heath, Suite IV: Barwick Green

spotify.name: Wood, Arthur: My Native Heath, Suite: IV. Barwick Green

playlist_tracks.connection: Classic BBC Radio 4 theme tunes: Chopin's "Minute Waltz" is the theme for "Just a Minute"; Arthur Wood's "Barwick Green" is the theme for "The Archers".

playlist_tracks.name: The Reason
playlist_tracks.connection: Producers: "Barwick Green" was produced (as a recording) by Sir George Martin, who also produced Celine Dion's "The Reason".

playlist_tracks.name: Miss Jamaica
playlist_tracks.connection: Celine Dion is beloved in Jamaica (honorary Jamaican) and Jimmy Cliff's "Miss Jamaica" is an ode to the country.

playlist_tracks.name: Rock On
playlist_tracks.connection: Bass guitar: the bass is barely audible in Jimmy Cliff's 1962 recording due to recording quality, so Guy chose "Rock On" which is built entirely around prominent bass guitar.

playlist_tracks.name: Finale of Symphony No 6 in B Minor

spotify.name: Symphony No. 6 in B Minor, Op. 74 "Pathétique": IV. Finale. Adagio lamentoso – Andante

playlist_tracks.connection: Heartbeat rhythm: the hypnotic heartbeat opening of "Rock On" connects to the heartbeat motif at the end of Tchaikovsky's Pathétique Symphony finale.
playlist_tracks.comments: Second time a heartbeat-related connection has been made (Mahler's arrhythmic heartbeat was used as a connection in series 8, episode 4).

playlist_tracks.name: Hockets for Two Voices: 1
playlist_tracks.connection: Hocketing technique: Tchaikovsky uses hocketing (alternating notes between first and second violins) in the Pathétique opening; Meara O'Reilly's "Hockets for Two Voices" is built entirely on this device.

playlist_tracks.name: Knee Play 5 from Einstein on the Beach

spotify.name: Einstein on the Beach: Knee Play 5

playlist_tracks.connection: The Kitchen (New York avant-garde arts centre): Meara O'Reilly performed "Hockets for Two Voices" there in 2016; Philip Glass developed and performed "Einstein on the Beach" (including "Knee Play 5") there in the 1970s.
Counting in songs: Philip Glass's "Knee Play 5" features counting (originally placeholders for lyrics); Manfred Mann's "5-4-3-2-1" counts down.

playlist_tracks.name: Dance of the Mirlitons from The Nutcracker

spotify.name: The Nutcracker Suite, Op. 71a: IIf. Dance of the Reed-Pipes

playlist_tracks.connection: Chocolate bar adverts: Manfred Mann's "5-4-3-2-1" was used in an advert for a chocolate bar of the same name; Tchaikovsky's "Dance of the Mirlitons" was used in Cadbury's Fruit and Nut adverts.

playlist_tracks.name: The Weary Blues
playlist_tracks.connection: Literature adapted into music: Tchaikovsky's "Nutcracker" is based on Hoffmann's story; Langston Hughes's "The Weary Blues" is a sonic adaptation of his own poem, with music by Charles Mingus.

playlist_tracks.name: Boda Negra
playlist_tracks.connection: Cultural movements celebrating Black heritage: the Harlem Renaissance (Langston Hughes) and the Cuban Negrismo movement (María Teresa Vera's "Boda Negra").

playlist_tracks.name: West End Girls
playlist_tracks.connection: Long-standing musical partnerships: María Teresa Vera's duo with Lorenzo Hierrezuelo lasted 27 years; Pet Shop Boys (Neil Tennant and Chris Lowe) have worked together continuously since 1981.

playlist_tracks.name: When Doves Cry
playlist_tracks.connection: Yamaha DX7 synthesizer: central to the sound of "West End Girls" and also featured prominently on Prince's "When Doves Cry".

playlist_tracks.name: Slap That Bass
playlist_tracks.connection: The bass: Prince famously removed the bass line from "When Doves Cry"; Ella Fitzgerald's "Slap That Bass" celebrates the instrument.

playlist_tracks.name: La Valse
playlist_tracks.connection: Gershwin and Ravel: Gershwin wanted to study with Ravel, who refused, saying "why become a second-rate Ravel when you're already a first-rate Gershwin?"; the track moves from Gershwin ("Slap That Bass") to Ravel ("La Valse").

playlist_tracks.name: Like Clockwork
playlist_tracks.connection: Ravel's fascination with mechanical clocks and timepieces links to The Boomtown Rats' "Like Clockwork", with its rhythmic, clock-like pulse.

playlist_tracks.name: Mundian To Bach Ke
playlist_tracks.connection: Songs that launched radio stations: "Like Clockwork" was the first track played on RTÉ Radio 2 in 1979; "Mundian To Bach Ke" was the first track played on the BBC Asian Network's digital relaunch in 2002.
